LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO THE OCT-14-2025 HEARING RE: PETITIONER RONALD RAMOS' HEARING ON VERIFIED PETITION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE UNOPPOSED PETITION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. PETITIONER ATTACHES UNAUTHENTICATED EXHIBITS TO THE PETITION AS A BASIS FOR ADJUDICATION. (SEE EVID. CODE, SECTIONS 1400 & 1401.) SETTING ASIDE THE UNAUTHENTICATED EXHIBITS, THIS COURT DOES NOT HAVE GENEALOGICAL EXPERTISE AND THUS HAS NO BASIS TO MAKE THE FINDINGS OF FACT THAT PETITIONER REQUESTS. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

LAW AND MOTION 301, PETITIONER RONALD JORDAN RAMOS'S VERIFIED AMENDED PETITION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. A REVIEW OF THE DOCUMENTS SUBMITTED BY PETITIONER SHOWS THAT PETITIONER IS ENTITLED TO JUDGMENT AS REQUESTED IN PARAGRAPH 20 OF HIS VERIFIED AMENDED PETITION. PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

calendar.matters:
Verified Amended Petition For Declaratory Relief
Off Calendar Apr-07-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER RONALD JORDAN RAMOS'S VERIFIED AMENDED PETITION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Petitioner Ronald Jordan Ramos's petition for declaratory relief is granted. A review of the documents submitted by petitioner shows that petitioner is entitled to judgment as requested in paragraph 20 of his verified amended petition. Proposed order submitted. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)

Added to LAW AND MOTION Calendar for Notice Of Hearing On Verified Petition For Declaratory Relief
Off Calendar Oct-14-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PETITIONER RONALD RAMOS' Hearing On Verified Petition For Declaratory Relief.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The unopposed petition for declaratory relief is denied without prejudice. Petitioner attaches unauthenticated exhibits to the petition as a basis for adjudication. (See Evid. Code, sections 1400 & 1401.) Setting aside the unauthenticated exhibits, this court does not have genealogical expertise and thus has no basis to make the findings of fact that petitioner requests.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
